retry_reason: Failure (NULL return) means either the object does not exist or its refcount is zero (object being freed). Retrying the same lookup without state changes is pointless; caller should handle absence appropriately and not retry the same unmodified lookup.
contract_summary: This function performs an RCU-protected lookup of a batadv_meshif_vlan object by VID and conditionally acquires a reference on it using kref_get_unless_zero. It returns the object with an elevated refcount if found and alive, or NULL otherwise. The caller must check the return value: a non-NULL pointer must later be released via a corresponding put (e.g., batadv_meshif_vlan_put). The RCU read lock is held only during the lookup, not after return, so the caller does not need to hold RCU lock after the call. This is a classic get-by-key wrapper that provides safe access to a shared, reference-counted object.
